name: UNIQLO
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of UNIQLO's brand language — the Japanese basics retailer founded on 'LifeWear' as a philosophy. Canvas is pure white. The signature is the UNIQLO red (#ff0000) used as a single chromatic asset on the wordmark, on price callouts, and on the rare hero CTA — a true #ff0000, not a brand-tinted variant. Type runs a dual Japanese-Latin system: Helvetica Neue Bold (Latin) and the proprietary UNIQLO Japanese face (Japanese), often locked side-by-side on the same page in a kana-and-latin co-typesetting that announces the brand's Tokyo origin. Photography is hyper-real flat-light fashion — models against pure white or against urban Tokyo backdrops — no styling theatrics, no cinematic grading.

design_language.motion_signature: Flat-light still photography is the visual anchor — motion is rare. Hero image carousels cross-fade slowly (~1.2s). Product tiles have zero hover transform. The only signature motion is the red-block dropdown animation in the mega-menu — a hard 200ms slide-down, no easing flourish.

trademark_signals: UNIQLO red (#ff0000) — pure red, not a tinted variant — used on the wordmark, on price-cut callouts, and on the rare hero CTA — never as a section background
Kana-and-Latin co-typesetting — Japanese characters and Latin headlines on the same page at matched scale, announcing the Tokyo origin
Flat-light fashion photography — every model shot on pure-white or pure-black background under flat soft light, no cinematic grading, no styling props — the garment is the point
Dense category-grid pages — 4-up product tiles with image + name + price, often 40-50 tiles per page, refusing the 'curated 6-product hero' convention
'LifeWear' editorial pages — long-scroll magazine-format features documenting fabric science, designer collaborations, and seasonal collections — written as essays, not as marketing
ai_slop_avoided: Don't tint the UNIQLO red. It is pure #ff0000 — any pinkening, muting, or warmth shifts off-brand
Don't style fashion photography with mood lighting or color grading. The brand is flat-light, hyper-real, no styling — the garment is the only signal
Don't drop the Japanese characters on global pages. The kana presence is part of the brand's Tokyo authenticity
Don't curate-down the product grid to 6 tiles. UNIQLO category pages are dense, catalog-style, and embrace abundance
Don't introduce a chromatic accent. Black + white + red is the trinity; teal/blue/green accents read as off-brand
